name: bc
description: Arbitrary-precision decimal arithmetic language and calculator. Evaluates mathematical expressions with unlimited precision. Supports command-line arguments for input files, expressions, and options like -c (digit clamping), -e (evaluate expression), -f (read file), -i (interactive mode), -s (scale), and -v (version). Use for complex calculations, financial computations, and scripting mathematical operations.

input.description: Optional command-line arguments for expressions, files, and options. The first argument is the expression to evaluate (via -e), followed by input files (via -f), and optional flags like -c, -s, -v. No standard input required.

output.description: Result of the evaluated expression(s). Returns a decimal number or an error message if the expression is invalid.
